articles.title: All the latest updates on AI data centers
articles.summary: Tech companies are rapidly expanding AI data centers as essential infrastructure, but face significant global opposition due to energy consumption, environmental concerns, and impacts on power grids and communities.

articles.title: PlayStation sees AI as a ‘powerful tool’ to help make games
articles.summary: Sony views AI as a powerful tool for game development while emphasizing that human vision, design, and emotional impact remain central to PlayStation games.

articles.title: Microsoft was worried OpenAI would run off to Amazon and ‘shit-talk’ Azure
articles.summary: Microsoft executives feared OpenAI might leave for Amazon and criticize Azure, according to court documents from the Musk v. Altman trial revealing early partnership discussions between Nadella and Altman in 2017.

articles.title: Laid-off Oracle workers tried to negotiate better severance. Oracle said no.
articles.summary: Oracle laid off workers who attempted to negotiate severance but were denied. Remote workers were excluded from WARN Act protections requiring two months' notice.

articles.title: Intel’s comeback story is even wilder than it seems
articles.summary: Intel's stock surged 490% in a year, but Wall Street's optimism may exceed the company's actual turnaround progress, suggesting the rally could be overheated.

articles.title: Cloudflare says AI made 1,100 jobs obsolete, even as revenue hit a record high
articles.summary: Cloudflare laid off 1,100 employees due to AI efficiency gains eliminating support roles, despite record revenue. CEO Matthew Prince attributed the cuts to artificial intelligence improving operational productivity.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS and Google Cloud. The investment capitalizes on developer frustration with legacy cloud complexity and costs.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code's $20-$200 monthly pricing has prompted developers to seek alternatives. Goose, a free open-source AI coding agent by Block, offers similar functionality without subscription fees or rate limits.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign using AI tokens and coding challenges. The startup's creative $5K marketing stunt decoded to a puzzle that attracted thousands of engineer applicants.

articles.title: Chaos erupts as cyberattack disrupts learning platform Canvas amid finals
articles.summary: A cyberattack disrupted Canvas learning platform during US finals, forcing schools offline. Instructure restored service Friday after identifying unauthorized network activity, with user data compromised but passwords and financial info unaffected.

articles.title: Mozilla says 271 vulnerabilities found by Mythos have "almost no false positives"
articles.summary: Mozilla used Anthropic's Mythos AI to identify 271 Firefox vulnerabilities with minimal false positives over two months. The breakthrough demonstrates AI-assisted vulnerability detection is becoming practically viable for real-world security applications.

articles.title: Ars Asks: Share your shell and show us your tricked-out terminals!
articles.summary: Terminal windows remain essential tools for developers, contrary to predictions that graphical interfaces would eliminate command-line usage. The article discusses why text-based interfaces are still superior for many computing tasks.

articles.title: Musk v. Altman week 2: OpenAI fires back, and Shivon Zilis reveals that Musk tried to poach Sam Altman
articles.summary: In week two of Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI, the company fired back, claiming Musk pushed for a for-profit structure and sued after losing control. Testimony revealed Musk allegedly tried to poach Sam Altman.

articles.title: Here’s what you need to know about the cruise ship hantavirus outbreak
articles.summary: Eight cruise ship passengers contracted hantavirus, a rare rat-transmitted virus with three deaths reported. The ship is docking in the Canary Islands for safe disembarkation as health experts assess the outbreak.

articles.title: The Download: AI malaise and babymaking tech
articles.summary: AI is pervasive but its societal impact remains unclear, causing widespread uncertainty about job displacement, economic effects, and optimal integration levels.

articles.title: CyberSecQwen-4B: Why Defensive Cyber Needs Small, Specialized, Locally-Runnable Models
articles.summary: CyberSecQwen-4B is a specialized small AI model designed for cybersecurity defense that runs locally without cloud dependency, offering faster threat detection and analysis.

articles.title: EMO: Pretraining mixture of experts for emergent modularity
articles.summary: EMO introduces a pretraining approach for mixture of experts models that develops emergent modularity. The method enables specialized expert modules to automatically develop during training without explicit supervision.

articles.title: vLLM V0 to V1: Correctness Before Corrections in RL
articles.summary: vLLM upgrades from V0 to V1, prioritizing correctness before implementing corrections in reinforcement learning. The update focuses on ensuring accurate model behavior as foundation for improvement.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and upcoming developments in the field, exploring what innovations and challenges lie ahead for the AI industry.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing strategies.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft has open-sourced its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural technology tools publicly available for developers and farmers to advance sustainable farming practices.

articles.title: See what happens when creative legends use AI to make ads for small businesses.
articles.summary: Google launches The Small Brief, pairing ad industry legends with local businesses to create innovative AI-powered advertisements. The initiative aims to showcase how creative professionals can leverage AI tools to help small businesses grow.

articles.title: 5 gardening tips you can try right in Search
articles.summary: Google has compiled five gardening tips accessible through its AI Mode, Search Live, and Shopping features to help users grow healthier plants using integrated tools.

articles.title: Google is partnering with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners on the $3.5 million Future Vision film competition.
articles.summary: Google partners with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners on a $3.5 million Future Vision film competition to create films exploring AI's potential impact on society and human experience.

articles.title: AlphaEvolve: How our Gemini-powered coding agent is scaling impact across fields
articles.summary: AlphaEvolve leverages Gemini-powered algorithms to create scalable impact across business, infrastructure, and scientific domains through advanced coding agents.

articles.title: Enabling a new model for healthcare with AI co-clinician
articles.summary: Researchers are developing an AI co-clinician to augment healthcare delivery. The project explores how AI can partner with medical professionals to improve patient care.

articles.title: Announcing our partnership with the Republic of Korea
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with South Korea to accelerate scientific breakthroughs using advanced AI models. The collaboration aims to leverage frontier artificial intelligence technology.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #340 - OpenAI vs Musk + Microsoft, DeepSeek v4, Vision Banana
articles.summary: Elon Musk's testimony dominated the first week of his trial against Sam Altman, focusing on allegations that OpenAI misappropriated charitable organization status. Evidence revealed Musk's xAI used OpenAI models for training.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #243 - GPT 5.5, DeepSeek V4, AI safety sabotage
articles.summary: The podcast discusses OpenAI's GPT-5.5 release with coding improvements and safety monitoring, xAI's Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0 for real-time voice agents, and recent AI safety concerns.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #242 - ChatGPT Images 2.0, Qwen 3.6 Max, Kimi-K2.6
articles.summary: Episode 242 discusses OpenAI's ChatGPT Images 2.0 with improved text rendering, Qwen 3.6 Max, and Kimi-K2.6. The new image model uses transformer architecture for agentic computer use capabilities.

articles.title: RingCentral adds Shopify, Calendly, and WhatsApp to AI Receptionist
articles.summary: RingCentral expanded its AI Receptionist with integrations for Shopify, Calendly, and WhatsApp, enabling it to handle order inquiries, appointments, and messages. Over 11,800 businesses now use the product for customer service tasks.

articles.title: AI helping ease the UK’s NHS burden
articles.summary: AI-enabled virtual care is being deployed in the UK NHS to reduce pressure on hospitals and manage the 7.25 million patient waiting list by shifting care into community settings.

articles.title: HP and the art of AI and data for the enterprise
articles.summary: HP discusses enterprise AI challenges, including leveraging first-party data effectively and deciding between cloud versus local compute solutions for AI models and data processing.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to identify which agents cause task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, improving debugging of collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture designed to improve autonomous robot navigation in complex indoor environments by addressing localization, self-positioning, and path planning challenges.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: How Memory Sparse Attention scales LLM memory to 100 million tokens
articles.summary: Memory Sparse Attention enables LLMs to process 100 million token context windows while maintaining accuracy, significantly expanding language model capabilities.

articles.title: Claude Code is leaking API keys into public package registries
articles.summary: A study reveals that Claude Code and similar AI coding assistants are inadvertently leaking sensitive API keys to public code repositories and package registries.

articles.title: Anthropic’s MCP vulnerability: When ‘expected behavior’ becomes a supply chain nightmare
articles.summary: Security researchers discovered a critical architectural flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol that exposes millions of AI applications to remote takeover attacks.

articles.title: 9 Best AI Tools for Spec-Driven Development in 2026: Kiro, BMAD, GSD, and More Compare
articles.summary: Spec-driven development addresses AI coding's speed-without-clarity problem by treating structured specifications as the source of truth. The article reviews 9 AI tools like AWS Kiro that developers use in 2026 to implement SDD workflows.

articles.title: Meet GitHub Spec-Kit: An Open Source Toolkit for Spec-Driven Development with AI Coding Agents
articles.summary: GitHub released Spec-Kit, an open-source toolkit promoting Spec-Driven Development to improve AI coding agents' reliability. It addresses the issue of vague prompts causing subtle code errors by treating agents as literal pair programmers needing precise specifications.

articles.title: OpenAI Adds Chrome Extension to Codex, Letting Its AI Agent Access LinkedIn, Salesforce, Gmail, and Internal Tools via Signed-In Sessions
articles.summary: OpenAI launched a Codex Chrome extension enabling its AI agent to access web-based tools like LinkedIn, Salesforce, and Gmail using signed-in browser sessions, filling gaps that APIs and plugins couldn't cover.

articles.title: The best 85-inch TVs in 2026: Expert recommended
articles.summary: Expert reviewers tested top 85-inch TVs from Samsung, Sony, and others to help create a cinematic home theater experience. These recommendations guide consumers in selecting the best large-screen models.

articles.title: Samsung watches can predict if you're about to faint - but there are big caveats
articles.summary: Samsung watches can predict fainting episodes using advanced sensors, potentially helping the 40% of people who experience syncope. However, significant limitations and caveats exist regarding accuracy and reliability.

articles.title: Best VPN services 2026: Expert tested and recommended
articles.summary: Article reviews top VPN services for 2026, tested and ranked for privacy protection, censorship avoidance, and bypassing age restrictions.

articles.title: Implementing Permission-Gated Tool Calling in Python Agents
articles.summary: AI agents now implement permission-gated tool calling in Python, enabling controlled access to functions. This advancement allows agents to operate beyond basic chatbot capabilities with security-focused authorization mechanisms.

articles.title: The Roadmap to Mastering Tool Calling in AI Agents
articles.summary: The article provides guidance on mastering tool calling capabilities in AI agents, enabling them to effectively integrate and utilize external tools for enhanced functionality and performance.

articles.title: Implementing Statistical Guardrails for Non-Deterministic Agents
articles.summary: The article discusses implementing statistical safeguards for non-deterministic AI agents, which produce varying outputs from identical inputs across multiple runs.

articles.title: RAG Is Blind to Time — I Built a Temporal Layer to Fix It in Production
articles.summary: RAG systems lack temporal awareness, retrieving outdated information. A developer built a temporal layer filtering expired facts and boosting time-sensitive signals to ensure current accuracy.

articles.title: From Data Scientist to AI Architect
articles.summary: The article discusses the evolution from traditional data science to AI architecture, marking a shift away from model-centric approaches in the field.

articles.title: The AI Agent Security Surface: What Gets Exposed When You Add Tools and Memory
articles.summary: AI agents with tools and memory create expanded security vulnerabilities beyond basic prompt attacks. A framework is needed to identify and mitigate backend attack vectors in agentic workflows.

articles.title: Powering the Next American Century: US Energy Secretary Chris Wright and NVIDIA’s Ian Buck on the Genesis Mission
articles.summary: US Energy Secretary Chris Wright and NVIDIA's Ian Buck argue that American AI leadership depends on energy leadership, with AI helping to build the energy infrastructure needed to power the nation's future.

articles.title: Linked and Loaded: Gaijin Single Sign-On Now Available on GeForce NOW
articles.summary: GeForce NOW now supports Gaijin Single Sign-On for faster logins to Gaijin games. Seven new games were added to the cloud gaming platform this week, enhancing instant access across devices.

articles.title: NVIDIA Spectrum-X — the Open, AI-Native Ethernet Fabric — Sets the Standard for Gigascale AI, Now With MRC
articles.summary: NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet fabric advances AI networking for gigascale deployments, adopted by OpenAI, Microsoft, and Oracle. The technology prioritizes performance, resilience, and scalability for AI infrastructure.

articles.title: Halliburton enhances seismic workflow creation with Amazon Bedrock and Generative AI
articles.summary: Halliburton partnered with AWS to develop an AI-powered assistant using Amazon Bedrock that simplifies seismic workflow creation, reducing manual configuration complexity and making the tool more accessible to energy exploration professionals.

articles.title: Secure short-term GPU capacity for ML workloads with EC2 Capacity Blocks for ML and SageMaker training plans
articles.summary: AWS introduces EC2 Capacity Blocks for ML and SageMaker training plans to address GPU supply shortages, enabling companies to secure reliable short-term GPU capacity for machine learning workloads.

articles.title: Overcoming reward signal challenges: Verifiable rewards-based reinforcement learning with GRPO on SageMaker AI
articles.summary: Training LLMs with reinforcement learning faces reward signal reliability challenges. RLVR introduces verification and transparency to improve training performance in verifiable domains like math and code generation.

articles.title: Improving Bash Generation in Small Language Models with Grammar-Constrained Decoding
articles.summary: Researchers improve Bash command generation in small language models using grammar-constrained decoding, enabling AI agents to produce valid shell commands for file operations and system interactions.

articles.title: Streaming Tokens and Tools: Multi-Turn Agentic Harness Support in NVIDIA Dynamo
articles.summary: NVIDIA Dynamo now supports streaming tokens and tools for multi-turn agentic interactions, enabling structured exchanges where assistants interleave reasoning with tool calls while preserving context.

articles.title: Achieving Peak System and Workload Efficiency on NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 with Slurm Block Scheduling
articles.summary: NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 extends NVLink coherence across entire racks for exascale performance, requiring new scheduling approaches like Slurm block scheduling to optimize system efficiency.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study examines how different generations perceive and respond to social media advertisements across various platforms, revealing generational differences in consumer attitudes toward ads.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing technology to reduce carbon footprint and sharing solutions openly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with an unconventional background leveraged diverse skills to develop innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how non-traditional paths can lead to breakthrough advancements in emerging tech fields.

articles.title: Adaptive Parallel Reasoning: The Next Paradigm in Efficient Inference Scaling
articles.summary: Adaptive Parallel Reasoning enables AI models to autonomously decide when to decompose tasks into parallel subtasks, determine concurrent thread counts, and coordinate them based on problem requirements for more efficient inference scaling.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for world models enabling long-horizon planning through parallel trajectory optimization, stochastic exploration, and gradient reshaping for robust action signals.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers are developing methods to identify and understand interactions at scale in Large Language Models to improve interpretability and AI transparency. This work aims to make LLM decision-making processes more transparent for safer, more trustworthy AI systems.
